2606.14179.title: CacheRL:Multi-Turn Tool-Calling Agents via Cached Rollouts and Hybrid Reward
2606.14179.paper_summary: We present CacheRL, a system for training small agent foundation models that achieves 92 percent process accuracy on multi-step tool-calling tasks, approaching GPT-5's 94 percent while requiring 100 times less compute. Our approach addresses three challenges in practical agent training: transferring tool-calling knowledge from large models at scale, enabling reinforcement learning without costly live tool execution, and learning robustly from noisy cached environments. CacheRL introduces three key innovations. First, a hybrid thinking trajectory pipeline augments agent trajectories with LLM-generated reasoning traces, producing training examples that teach models not only what tools to call but also why. Second, the CacheAgentLoop eliminates live execution costs through a three-tier fuzzy cache while preserving trajectory fidelity using token-level masking. Third, a cache-tier-aware reward dynamically adjusts answer-quality weights to avoid penalizing models for cache-induced limitations. Through iterative supervised fine-tuning (SFT) and Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO), CacheRL improves Qwen3-4B-Thinking's validation reward from 0.43 to 0.78. On public agentic tool-calling benchmarks, our model achieves competitive performance against frontier models such as GPT-5. Ablation studies show that removing knowledge transfer reduces performance by 41 percent, while cache-aware rewards contribute a 17 percent improvement. Interestingly, reinforcement learning improves training stability but yields limited gains beyond strong supervised fine-tuning, suggesting that data quality and reward design play a more important role than complex optimization methods in building practical small agent models.
2606.14179.paper_summary_zh: 我們提出了CacheRL，一個用於訓練小型代理基礎模型的系統，在多步驟工具調用任務中達到92%的過程準確率，接近GPT-5的94%，同時所需計算量減少100倍。我們的方法解決了實際代理訓練中的三個挑戰：從大型模型中大規模轉移工具調用知識、在不需要昂貴的實時工具執行的情況下啟用強化學習，以及從噪聲緩存環境中穩健學習。CacheRL引入了三個關鍵創新。首先，一個混合思維軌跡管道通過LLM生成的推理痕跡增強代理軌跡，生成的訓練示例不僅教會模型調用哪些工具，還教會為什麼要這樣做。其次，CacheAgentLoop通過三層模糊緩存消除了實時執行成本，同時使用令牌級掩碼保持軌跡的真實性。第三，緩存層感知的獎勵動態調整答案質量權重，以避免因緩存引起的限制而懲罰模型。通過迭代的監督微調（SFT）和群體相對策略優化（GRPO），CacheRL將Qwen3-4B-Thinking的驗證獎勵從0.43提高到0.78。在公共代理工具調用基準上，我們的模型在與前沿模型如GPT-5的競爭中表現出色。消融研究表明，移除知識轉移會使性能降低41%，而緩存感知獎勵則貢獻了17%的改進。有趣的是，強化學習提高了訓練穩定性，但在強監督微調之外的收益有限，這表明數據質量和獎勵設計在構建實用的小型代理模型中比複雜的優化方法更為重要。
2606.14179.author: Md Amirul Islam et.al.
2606.14179.authors: Md Amirul Islam, Sumiran Thakur, Huancheng Chen, Su Min Park, Jiayun Wang, Gyuhak Kim
